title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. monticola
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. monticola, also known as the Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and wounds, due to its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Alpine Lady's Mantle often involve infusions or tinctures made from the leaves and flowers, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
